name: CheckBoxControl
description: A checkbox control for use within DFDialog forms. Wraps the Gravity UI Checkbox component and integrates with react-final-form via a normalized value/onChange interface.
when_to_use: When a dialog form field needs a boolean yes/no toggle
When registering a custom control of type 'checkbox' in DFDialog fields
avoid: Do not use standalone outside DFDialog without the ControlProps interface — use Gravity UI Checkbox directly instead
Do not use for multi-select scenarios — use SelectControl instead

props.name: value
props.description: Current checked state of the checkbox.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback called with the new boolean value when checkbox state changes.

name: CollapsibleSection
description: A section container with a captioned header that can be collapsed or expanded. Supports optional always-expanded mode and configurable initial collapsed state.
when_to_use: When grouping related dialog fields under a labelled section that users can hide
When a form has many fields and some groups should be initially collapsed to reduce visual noise
avoid: Do not use for top-level page layout sections — intended for dialog/form grouping
Do not use when the section must always stay open and has no toggle need — just use a plain div or DFDialog section type instead

props.name: caption
props.description: Header text displayed for the section.

props.name: alwaysExpanded
props.description: When true, disables the toggle and the section stays permanently open.

props.name: captionClassName
props.description: Additional CSS class applied to the caption element.

props.name: children
props.description: Section content.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class applied to the root element.

props.name: initialCollapsed
props.description: When true, the section starts in a collapsed state.

name: CustomBlock
description: A passthrough control that renders arbitrary React content as a dialog field. Allows embedding any ReactNode inside a DFDialog field slot without field binding.
when_to_use: When a form needs to display non-interactive content or a custom UI widget inside a DFDialog field row
When building a field of type 'block' in DFDialog fields array
avoid: Do not use when the embedded content needs to read or write form values — use a registered custom control via registerDialogControl instead

props.name: children
props.description: Content to render inside the dialog field slot.

props.name: onChange
props.description: No-op change handler required by the ControlProps interface.

props.name: value
props.description: Unused value binding required by the ControlProps interface.

examples: <DFDialog fields={[{ name: 'info', type: 'block', extras: { children: <Alert message="Read this carefully" /> } }]} />

name: DFDialog
description: A form dialog component backed by react-final-form that renders typed field controls in a tabbed or single-page layout. Supports built-in control types (text, checkbox, select, etc.), field-level and form-level validation, visibility conditions, linked fields, collapsible sections, virtualized tabs, and cloneable tabs.
when_to_use: When building a modal or inline form with multiple typed fields and validation
When a form needs tabbed navigation between groups of fields
When fields need to show/hide based on values of other fields
When form values must be validated at field-level or form-level before submission
avoid: Do not use for simple single-field confirmations — use a plain Gravity UI Dialog with a single input
Do not use when the form state is already managed externally by a non-final-form solution

props.name: fields
props.description: Field descriptors array. Can be flat (single tab) or an array of tab objects each containing a fields sub-array.

props.name: onAdd
props.type: (form: FormApi<FormValues>) => Promise<{ validationErrors?: ValidationErrors } | undefined | void>
props.description: Called when the Apply button is clicked. Receives the final-form FormApi. Return rejected promise or validationErrors to surface form errors.

props.name: visible
props.description: Controls dialog visibility.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element.

props.name: disableFormReset
props.description: When true, the form is not reset after a successful submit.

props.name: footerProps
props.type: { hidden?: boolean; textApply?: React.ReactNode; textCancel?: string; content?: React.ReactNode; propsButtonCancel?: DialogFooterProps['propsButtonCancel']; propsButtonApply?: DialogFooterProps['propsButtonApply'] }
props.description: Customises the dialog footer: button labels, extra content, or hidden state.

props.name: formExtras
props.description: Additional react-final-form FormProps passed directly to the Form component.

props.name: formId
props.description: HTML form id attribute for the underlying form element.

props.name: headerProps
props.type: { title?: React.ReactNode; insertAfter?: React.ReactNode; insertBefore?: React.ReactNode }
props.description: Dialog header configuration: title and optional content slots.

props.name: initialValues
props.description: Initial field values. If omitted, each registered control's getDefaultValue is used.

props.name: isApplyDisabled
props.description: Callback that disables the Apply button based on current form state.

props.name: modal
props.description: When true renders in a modal overlay; when false renders inline.

props.name: onActiveTabChange
props.description: Called whenever the active tab changes.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called when the dialog is closed or Cancel is clicked.

props.name: pristineSubmittable
props.description: Allows submitting the form even when no fields have been changed.

props.name: size
props.description: Dialog size.

props.name: validate
props.description: Form-level validation function. Receives all values and returns an error map.

props.name: virtualized
props.description: Enables virtualized rendering for dialogs with very many tabs.

props.name: waitingMessage
props.description: Message or element shown while the onAdd promise is pending.

examples:
<DFDialog<FormValues>
  visible={open}
  headerProps={{ title: 'Create user' }}
  onAdd={(form) => {
    console.log(form.getState().values);
    return Promise.resolve();
  }}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
  fields={[
    { name: 'firstName', type: 'text', caption: 'First name', required: true },
    { name: 'lastName', type: 'text', caption: 'Last name' },
  ]}
/>

<DFDialog<FormValues>
  visible={open}
  modal
  headerProps={{ title: 'Settings' }}
  onAdd={handleSubmit}
  fields={[
    { name: 'tab1', type: 'tab-vertical', title: 'General', fields: [
      { name: 'name', type: 'text', caption: 'Name' },
    ]},
    { name: 'tab2', type: 'tab-vertical', title: 'Advanced', fields: [
      { name: 'debug', type: 'tumbler', caption: 'Debug mode' },
    ]},
  ]}
/>

name: EditableList
description: A list of string items where each item can be individually toggled for removal. Supports frozen (read-only) items, custom item renderers, and a show-more/show-less toggle when items exceed a visible count limit.
when_to_use: When a dialog field represents a list of existing strings where users can mark items for removal
When used as the built-in 'editable-list' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use when users need to add new items — EditableList only supports removal toggling, not creation
Do not use for multiple grouped lists — use EditableManyLists instead

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the updated items array when an item's removed state is toggled.

props.name: value
props.description: Current array of list items. Each item has title, optional removed, frozen, and data fields.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element.

props.name: frozen
props.description: When true, all items are read-only and the remove action is hidden.

props.name: itemClassName
props.description: Additional CSS class applied to each item element.

props.name: itemRenderer
props.description: Custom render function for each item. Defaults to rendering item.title.

props.name: maxVisibleCount
props.description: Maximum number of items to show before a show-all toggle appears.

examples:
<EditableList
  value={[
    { title: 'item-1' },
    { title: 'item-2', frozen: true },
  ]}
  onChange={handleChange}
/>

name: EditableManyLists
description: A grouped editable list component that renders multiple titled EditableList instances side by side. Each group has its own title and array of removable items.
when_to_use: When a dialog field must display multiple named groups of removable items
When used as the built-in 'multi-editable-list' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use when only a single flat list is needed — use EditableList instead
Do not use when users need to add new items rather than just remove existing ones

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the full updated groups array when any item is toggled.

props.name: value
props.description: Array of group objects, each with a title string and a data array of EditableListItemType.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element.

props.name: itemRenderer
props.description: Custom renderer for individual items, passed through to each EditableList.

props.name: maxVisibleCount
props.description: Maximum visible items per list before a show-all toggle appears.

props.name: titleClassName
props.description: Additional CSS class applied to each group title element.

examples:
<EditableManyLists
  value={[
    { title: 'Admins', data: [{ title: 'alice' }, { title: 'bob' }] },
    { title: 'Viewers', data: [{ title: 'carol' }] },
  ]}
  onChange={handleChange}
/>

name: FocusBlurContainer
description: A container that tracks aggregate focus and blur state across all child elements. Fires onFocus when any child gains focus and onBlur when focus leaves the entire container.
when_to_use: When a composite input component needs a single onBlur callback that fires only when the user fully leaves the widget (not when tabbing between inner elements)
avoid: Do not use for simple single-element inputs — native onBlur is sufficient
Do not use as a focus trap for accessibility dialogs — use dedicated modal focus-trap utilities

props.name: children
props.description: Child elements whose collective focus state is tracked.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root div.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Called when focus leaves all children.

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Called when any child gains focus.

name: MultiTextControl
description: A tag-input control that lets users type and confirm multiple string values as labelled chips. Items are added on Enter or blur and can be individually removed. Wraps a Gravity UI TextInput with an adjacent LabelsGroup chip display.
when_to_use: When a form field requires an array of user-entered strings (e.g., email list, tags, aliases)
When used as the built-in 'multi-text' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use when items come from a predefined set — use SelectControl with multiple selection instead
Do not use when order or duplicates must be preserved with special logic

props.name: onChange
props.type: (value: Array<{ name: string; isDefault?: boolean }>) => void
props.description: Called with the updated items array when an item is added or removed.

props.name: value
props.description: Current array of label objects.

props.name: error
props.description: Validation error message to display.

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Called when focus leaves the entire control.

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Called when the control gains focus.

name: PlainText
description: A read-only display control that renders a string value as plain text inside a dialog field row. Shows an optional placeholder when the value is empty.
when_to_use: When a dialog field should display a computed or fixed value without allowing edits
When used as the built-in 'plain' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use when the text should be editable — use TextControl instead
Do not use for long descriptive paragraphs — use CustomBlock with a Text component instead

props.name: onChange
props.description: No-op change handler required by ControlProps interface.

props.name: value
props.description: The string value to display.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Content shown when value is empty.

name: RadioButtonControl
description: A segmented radio group control for selecting one value from a predefined options list. Wraps the Gravity UI SegmentedRadioGroup and normalizes the onChange interface for use with DFDialog.
when_to_use: When a dialog field requires selecting exactly one option from a small set of predefined values
When used as the built-in 'radio' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use when the option set is large — use SelectControl for dropdown selection
Do not use for boolean toggles — use TumblerControl or CheckBoxControl instead

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the new selected value string when selection changes.

props.name: value
props.description: Currently selected value.

props.name: options
props.description: Array of selectable options. Defaults to empty array.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the radio group. Defaults to 'm'.

examples:
<RadioButtonControl
  value={mode}
  onChange={setMode}
  options={[
    { value: 'read', label: 'Read' },
    { value: 'write', label: 'Write' },
  ]}
/>

name: SelectControl
description: A dropdown select control that wraps the Gravity UI Select component and normalizes the onChange callback for use with DFDialog form fields.
when_to_use: When a dialog field needs a dropdown list for selecting one or more values from a predefined set
When used as the built-in 'select' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use for small fixed sets of 2-4 options — RadioButtonControl is more discoverable
Do not use when users need to type arbitrary values — use MultiTextControl instead

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the new selected value(s) array when selection changes.

props.name: value
props.description: Currently selected values array.

examples:
<SelectControl
  value={selected}
  onChange={setSelected}
  options={[
    { value: 'opt1', content: 'Option 1' },
    { value: 'opt2', content: 'Option 2' },
  ]}
/>

name: TabField
description: A horizontal tab bar component used inside DFDialog for switching between tabbed content panes. Supports cloneable (multiple) tabs with per-tab remove buttons and validation error indicators.
when_to_use: When DFDialog needs horizontal tab navigation between groups of fields
Registered as the 'tab' control type inside DFDialog fields to enable tabbed layouts
avoid: Do not use directly as a standalone page-level navigation component — use Gravity UI Tabs instead
Do not use when vertical sidebar navigation is preferred — use TabFieldVertical instead

props.name: setActiveTab
props.description: Callback to update the currently active tab by id.

props.name: tabItems
props.description: Array of tab descriptor objects each with id, name, title, and optional error/removable/multiple flags.

props.name: activeTab
props.description: Id of the currently active tab.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the tab bar.

props.name: onCreateTab
props.type: (fieldName: string, active?: boolean, opts?: { userOptions?: any }) => void
props.description: Called when a new cloneable tab should be created.

props.name: onRemoveTab
props.description: Called when a removable cloneable tab's close button is clicked.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the tab bar.

examples:
<TabField
  tabItems={[
    { id: 'tab1', name: 'tab1', title: 'General' },
    { id: 'tab2', name: 'tab2', title: 'Advanced' },
  ]}
  activeTab={activeTab}
  setActiveTab={setActiveTab}
/>

name: TabFieldVertical
description: A vertical sidebar tab list variant of TabField that renders tabs as a scrollable vertical list using Gravity UI List. Supports sorting, virtualization, and custom item wrapping.
when_to_use: When DFDialog needs vertical left-side tab navigation between groups of fields
When the number of tabs is large and a sidebar navigation pattern is more appropriate than a horizontal bar
avoid: Do not use for horizontal tab navigation — use TabField instead
Do not use as a standalone sidebar navigation component — it is coupled to DFDialog internal TabItem data shapes

props.name: setActiveTab
props.description: Callback to update the currently active tab by id.

props.name: tabItems
props.description: Array of tab descriptor objects.

props.name: activeTab
props.description: Id of the currently active tab.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element.

props.name: onOrderChanged
props.description: Called when sortable tabs are reordered.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of each tab row. Defaults to 's'.

props.name: sortable
props.description: Enables drag-and-drop sorting of tabs.

props.name: virtualized
props.description: Enables virtualized rendering for large tab lists.

props.name: wrapTo
props.description: Optional wrapper around each tab item node.

name: TextAreaControl
description: A multi-line text area control that wraps the Gravity UI TextArea component and normalizes the value/onChange interface for use with DFDialog form fields.
when_to_use: When a dialog field needs multi-line text input (e.g., descriptions, notes, JSON snippets)
When used as the built-in 'textarea' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use for single-line input — use TextControl instead
Do not use for code with syntax highlighting — use a custom block with a code editor

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the new string value on each text change.

props.name: value
props.description: Current string value of the text area.

props.name: error
props.description: Validation error message to display.

name: TextControl
description: A single-line text input control that wraps the Gravity UI TextInput with a clear button and normalizes the value/onChange interface for use with DFDialog form fields.
when_to_use: When a dialog field needs single-line free-form text input
When used as the built-in 'text' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use for multi-line input — use TextAreaControl instead
Do not use for entering multiple values — use MultiTextControl instead

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the new string value on each change.

props.name: value
props.description: Current string value.

props.name: error
props.description: Validation error message to display.

name: TumblerControl
description: A toggle switch control that wraps the Gravity UI Switch component and normalizes the boolean value/onChange interface for DFDialog form fields. Supports optional left and right labels flanking the switch.
when_to_use: When a dialog field needs an on/off toggle for enabling or disabling a feature
When used as the built-in 'tumbler' field type in DFDialog
avoid: Do not use when the semantics are about agreement or selection — use CheckBoxControl instead
Do not use for switching between more than two states — use RadioButtonControl instead

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with the new boolean value when the switch is toggled.

props.name: value
props.description: Current switch state.

props.name: labelLeft
props.description: Optional label text displayed to the left of the switch.

props.name: labelRight
props.description: Optional label text displayed to the right of the switch.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the switch. Defaults to 'm'.

name: configure
description: Configures global settings for the dialog-fields library. Currently supports setting the display language ('en' or 'ru') for built-in i18n strings.
when_to_use: At application startup to set the language for DFDialog built-in labels and messages
avoid: Do not call repeatedly at runtime — intended for one-time app initialization

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Configuration object. lang sets the display language.
